message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="luca-palmieri" color="blue" summary="Both blocking items already fixed, please review Step 7 instead">
Heydon, both of your blocking items are already resolved in the current codebase. This review is for Step 6 (Projects), which already has 9/9 consensus and is committed.

**B1 (focus management)**: Already fixed. `templates/project_detail.html` line 10 loads `project-detail-focus.js`:
```html
<script src="/static/js/project-detail-focus.js"></script>
```

**B2 (stalled flag contrast)**: Already fixed. `static/css/main.css` line 1868 uses `color: var(--color-fg)`, not `var(--color-warning)`:
```css
.project-item__stalled-flag {
    color: var(--color-fg);
    background-color: var(--color-warning-light);
}
```

We are currently reviewing **Step 7 (Clarify as Project)**. The Step 7 changes are:

- `src/services/inbox_service.rs` lines 169-251: new `clarify_as_project()` service with transaction
- `src/routes/inbox.rs` lines 207-289: updated clarify handler branching on `clarify_type`
- `templates/inbox_item.html`: radio buttons (Next Action / Project), first action input with label, fieldset with legend for accessible grouping
- `static/css/main.css` lines 1226-1305: progressive disclosure via `:has(input[value="project"]:checked)` -- project fields hidden until "Project" radio selected
- `tests/api/inbox.rs` lines 836-1143: 8 new integration tests

Your accessibility expertise is especially needed on the template changes -- the radio button grouping, the conditionally-revealed first action input, and the fieldset/legend pattern. Could you review and provide your Step 7 verdict?
</teammate-message>

content.text: Let me review the Step 7 implementation files that Luca listed.

content.text: Good. Now let me check the route handler to verify server-side validation.

content.content:
207→#[derive(serde::Deserialize)] 208→pub struct ClarifyForm { 209→ pub context_id: Uuid, 210→ #[serde(default)] 211→ pub clarify_type: String, 212→ #[serde(default)] 213→ pub first_action_title: String, 214→} 215→ 216→pub async fn post_clarify_inbox_item( 217→ AuthenticatedUser(user_id): AuthenticatedUser, 218→ State(pool): State, 219→ headers: HeaderMap, 220→ Path(item_id): Path, 221→ Form(form): Form, 222→) -> Result { 223→ let htmx = is_htmx_request(&headers); 224→ let inbox_item_id = InboxItemId::from_uuid(item_id); 225→ let context_id = ContextId::from_uuid(form.context_id); 226→ 227→ if form.clarify_type == "project" { 228→ match clarify_as_project( 229→ &pool, 230→ &inbox_item_id, 231→ &user_id, 232→ context_id, 233→ form.first_action_title, 234→ ) 235→ .await 236→ { 237→ Ok(_result) => { 238→ if htmx { 239→ Ok(htmx_response_with_announce( 240→ Html(String::new()), 241→ "Clarified as project", 242→ )) 243→ } else { 244→ Ok(Redirect::to("/inbox").into_response()) 245→ } 246→ } 247→ Err(ClarifyAsProjectError::NotFound) => Ok(( 248→ StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, 249→ Html("

Inbox item not found

".to_string()), 250→ ) 251→ .into_response()), 252→ Err(ClarifyAsProjectError::Unauthorized) => Ok(( 253→ StatusCode::FORBIDDEN, 254→ Html("

Not authorized

".to_string()), 255→ ) 256→ .into_response()), 257→ Err(ClarifyAsProjectError::InvalidTitle(_)) => Ok(( 258→ StatusCode::UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY, 259→ Html("

First action title is required for projects

".to_string()), 260→ ) 261→ .into_response()), 262→ Err(ClarifyAsProjectError::Unexpected(err)) => Err(InboxError::Unexpected(err)), 263→ } 264→ } else { 265→ match clarify_as_next_action(&pool, &inbox_item_id, &user_id, context_id).await { 266→ Ok(_action) => { 267→ if htmx { 268→ Ok(htmx_response_with_announce( 269→ Html(String::new()), 270→ "Clarified as next action", 271→ )) 272→ } else { 273→ Ok(Redirect::to("/inbox").into_response()) 274→ } 275→ } 276→ Err(ClarifyAsNextActionError::NotFound) => Ok(( 277→ StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, 278→ Html("

Inbox item not found

".to_string()), 279→ ) 280→ .into_response()), 281→ Err(ClarifyAsNextActionError::Unauthorized) => Ok(( 282→ StatusCode::FORBIDDEN, 283→ Html("

Not authorized

".to_string()), 284→ ) 285→ .into_response()), 286→ Err(ClarifyAsNextActionError::Unexpected(err)) => Err(InboxError::Unexpected(err)), 287→ } 288→ } 289→} 290→ 291→#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]

169→#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
   170→pub enum ClarifyAsProjectError {
   171→    #[error("Inbox item not found")]
   172→    NotFound,
   173→    #[error("Not authorized to clarify this inbox item")]
   174→    Unauthorized,
   175→    #[error("Invalid first action title: {0}")]
   176→    InvalidTitle(#[from] TodoTitleError),
   177→    #[error(transparent)]
   178→    Unexpected(#[from] anyhow::Error),
   179→}
   180→
   181→pub struct ClarifyAsProjectResult {
   182→    pub project: Project,
   183→    pub first_action: NextAction,
   184→}
   185→
   186→#[tracing::instrument(
   187→    name = "clarify_as_project",
   188→    skip(pool, first_action_title),
   189→    fields(item_id = %item_id.as_uuid(), user_id = %user_id.as_uuid(), context_id = %context_id.as_uuid())
   190→)]
   191→pub async fn clarify_as_project(
   192→    pool: &PgPool,
   193→    item_id: &InboxItemId,
   194→    user_id: &UserId,
   195→    context_id: ContextId,
   196→    first_action_title: String,
   197→) -> Result<ClarifyAsProjectResult, ClarifyAsProjectError> {
   198→    let first_action_title = TodoTitle::parse(first_action_title)?;
   199→
   200→    let mut tx = pool.begin().await.map_err(|e| {
   201→        ClarifyAsProjectError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to begin transaction: {e}"))
   202→    })?;
   203→
   204→    let item = inbox_repository::find_inbox_item_by_id(&mut *tx, item_id)
   205→        .await
   206→        .map_err(|e| ClarifyAsProjectError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Database error: {e}")))?
   207→        .ok_or(ClarifyAsProjectError::NotFound)?;
   208→
   209→    if item.user_id() != user_id {
   210→        return Err(ClarifyAsProjectError::Unauthorized);
   211→    }
   212→
   213→    let project = Project::new(user_id.clone(), item.title().clone());
   214→
   215→    project_repository::insert_project(&mut *tx, &project)
   216→        .await
   217→        .map_err(|e| {
   218→            ClarifyAsProjectError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to insert project: {e}"))
   219→        })?;
   220→
   221→    let first_action = NextAction::new_for_project(
   222→        user_id.clone(),
   223→        context_id,
   224→        project.id().clone(),
   225→        first_action_title,
   226→    );
   227→
   228→    next_action_repository::insert_next_action(&mut *tx, &first_action)
   229→        .await
   230→        .map_err(|e| {
   231→            ClarifyAsProjectError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!(
   232→                "Failed to insert first next action: {e}"
   233→            ))
   234→        })?;
   235→
   236→    inbox_repository::delete_inbox_item(&mut *tx, item_id)
   237→        .await
   238→        .map_err(|e| {
   239→            ClarifyAsProjectError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to delete inbox item: {e}"))
   240→        })?;
   241→
   242→    tx.commit().await.map_err(|e| {
   243→        ClarifyAsProjectError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to commit transaction: {e}"))
   244→    })?;
   245→
   246→    tracing::info!("Inbox item clarified as project");
   247→    Ok(ClarifyAsProjectResult {
   248→        project,
   249→        first_action,
   250→    })
   251→}
   252→

content.text: Good. Now let me check the full CSS section for the inbox item styles and verify the integration tests cover the accessibility patterns.

content.content:
836→// ---- Clarify as Project ----
   837→
   838→#[tokio::test]
   839→async fn clarify_inbox_item_as_project_removes_from_inbox() {
   840→    let app = spawn_app().await;
   841→    let client = register_and_login(
   842→        &app.address,
   843→        "projclarify1@example.com",
   844→        "securepassword123",
   845→    )
   846→    .await;
   847→
   848→    let context_id = get_first_context_id(&client, &app.address).await;
   849→
   850→    // Capture an item
   851→    client
   852→        .post(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address))
   853→        .form(&[("title", "Build website")])
   854→        .send()
   855→        .await
   856→        .expect("Failed to capture");
   857→
   858→    let item_id = get_first_inbox_item_id(&client, &app.address).await;
   859→
   860→    // Clarify as project
   861→    let response = client
   862→        .post(format!("{}/inbox/{}/clarify", &app.address, item_id))
   863→        .form(&[
   864→            ("clarify_type", "project"),
   865→            ("context_id", &context_id),
   866→            ("first_action_title", "Set up hosting"),
   867→        ])
   868→        .send()
   869→        .await
   870→        .expect("Failed to clarify as project");
   871→
   872→    assert_eq!(
   873→        response.status().as_u16(),
   874→        303,
   875→        "Clarify as project should redirect"
   876→    );
   877→
   878→    // Item should be gone from inbox
   879→    let response = client
   880→        .get(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address))
   881→        .send()
   882→        .await
   883→        .expect("Failed to GET /inbox");
   884→    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
   885→    assert!(
   886→        !body.contains("Build website"),
   887→        "Clarified item should no longer appear in inbox"
   888→    );
   889→}
   890→
   891→#[tokio::test]
   892→async fn clarify_inbox_item_as_project_creates_project_and_first_action() {
   893→    let app = spawn_app().await;
   894→    let client = register_and_login(
   895→        &app.address,
   896→        "projclarify2@example.com",
   897→        "securepassword123",
   898→    )
   899→    .await;
   900→
   901→    let context_id = get_first_context_id(&client, &app.address).await;
   902→
   903→    // Capture an item
   904→    client
   905→        .post(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address))
   906→        .form(&[("title", "Plan vacation")])
   907→        .send()
   908→        .await
   909→        .expect("Failed to capture");
   910→
   911→    let item_id = get_first_inbox_item_id(&client, &app.address).await;
   912→
   913→    // Clarify as project
   914→    client
   915→        .post(format!("{}/inbox/{}/clarify", &app.address, item_id))
   916→        .form(&[
   917→            ("clarify_type", "project"),
   918→            ("context_id", &context_id),
   919→            ("first_action_title", "Research destinations"),
   920→        ])
   921→        .send()
   922→        .await
   923→        .expect("Failed to clarify as project");
   924→
   925→    // Project should appear in projects list with inbox item's title
   926→    let response = client
   927→        .get(format!("{}/projects", &app.address))
   928→        .send()
   929→        .await
   930→        .expect("Failed to GET /projects");
   931→    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
   932→    assert!(
   933→        body.contains("Plan vacation"),
   934→        "Project should appear in projects list with the inbox item's title"
   935→    );

936→
   937→    // First action should appear in next actions list
   938→    let response = client
   939→        .get(format!("{}/next-actions", &app.address))
   940→        .send()
   941→        .await
   942→        .expect("Failed to GET /next-actions");
   943→    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
   944→    assert!(
   945→        body.contains("Research destinations"),
   946→        "First action should appear in next actions list"
   947→    );
   948→}
   949→
   950→#[tokio::test]
   951→async fn clarify_as_project_with_empty_first_action_title_returns_422() {
   952→    let app = spawn_app().await;
   953→    let client = register_and_login(
   954→        &app.address,
   955→        "projclarify3@example.com",
   956→        "securepassword123",
   957→    )
   958→    .await;
   959→
   960→    let context_id = get_first_context_id(&client, &app.address).await;
   961→
   962→    // Capture an item
   963→    client
   964→        .post(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address))
   965→        .form(&[("title", "Needs first action")])
   966→        .send()
   967→        .await
   968→        .expect("Failed to capture");
   969→
   970→    let item_id = get_first_inbox_item_id(&client, &app.address).await;
   971→
   972→    // Clarify as project with empty first action title
   973→    let response = client
   974→        .post(format!("{}/inbox/{}/clarify", &app.address, item_id))
   975→        .form(&[
   976→            ("clarify_type", "project"),
   977→            ("context_id", &context_id),
   978→            ("first_action_title", ""),
   979→        ])
   980→        .send()
   981→        .await
   982→        .expect("Failed to clarify as project");
   983→
   984→    assert_eq!(
   985→        response.status().as_u16(),
   986→        422,
   987→        "Empty first action title should return 422"
   988→    );
   989→
   990→    // Item should still be in inbox
   991→    let response = client
   992→        .get(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address))
   993→        .send()
   994→        .await
   995→        .expect("Failed to GET /inbox");
   996→    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
   997→    assert!(
   998→        body.contains("Needs first action"),
   999→        "Item should still be in inbox after failed clarify"
  1000→    );
  1001→}
  1002→
  1003→#[tokio::test]
  1004→async fn clarify_nonexistent_inbox_item_as_project_returns_404() {
  1005→    let app = spawn_app().await;
  1006→    let client = register_and_login(
  1007→        &app.address,
  1008→        "projclarify4@example.com",
  1009→        "securepassword123",
  1010→    )
  1011→    .await;
  1012→
  1013→    let context_id = get_first_context_id(&client, &app.address).await;
  1014→    let fake_id = uuid::Uuid::new_v4();
  1015→
  1016→    let response = client
  1017→        .post(format!("{}/inbox/{}/clarify", &app.address, fake_id))
  1018→        .form(&[
  1019→            ("clarify_type", "project"),
  1020→            ("context_id", &context_id),
  1021→            ("first_action_title", "Some action"),
  1022→        ])
  1023→        .send()
  1024→        .await
  1025→        .expect("Failed to clarify");
  1026→
  1027→    assert_eq!(response.status().as_u16(), 404);
  1028→}
  1029→
  1030→#[tokio::test]
  1031→async fn htmx_clarify_as_project_returns_empty_body_with_announce() {
  1032→    let app = spawn_app().await;
  1033→    let client = register_and_login(
  1034→        &app.address,
  1035→        "projclarify5@example.com",
  1036→        "securepassword123",
  1037→    )
  1038→    .await;
  1039→
  1040→    let context_id = get_first_context_id(&client, &app.address).await;
  1041→
  1042→    client
  1043→        .post(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address))
  1044→        .form(&[("title", "HTMX project test")])
  1045→        .send()
  1046→        .await
  1047→        .expect("Failed to capture");
  1048→
  1049→    let item_id = get_first_inbox_item_id(&client, &app.address).await;
  1050→
  1051→    let response = client
  1052→        .post(format!("{}/inbox/{}/clarify", &app.address, item_id))
  1053→        .header("hx-request", "true")
  1054→        .form(&[
  1055→            ("clarify_type", "project"),
  1056→            ("context_id", &context_id),
  1057→            ("first_action_title", "First step"),
  1058→        ])
  1059→        .send()
  1060→        .await
  1061→        .expect("Failed to clarify");
  1062→
  1063→    assert_eq!(response.status().as_u16(), 200);
  1064→
  1065→    let trigger = response
  1066→        .headers()
  1067→        .get("hx-trigger")
  1068→        .expect("Missing HX-Trigger")
  1069→        .to_str()
  1070→        .unwrap();
  1071→    assert!(
  1072→        trigger.contains("Clarified as project"),
  1073→        "HX-Trigger should contain 'Clarified as project' announce"
  1074→    );
  1075→
  1076→    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
  1077→    assert!(
  1078→        body.is_empty(),
  1079→        "HTMX clarify as project should return empty body (item removed)"
  1080→    );
  1081→}
  1082→
  1083→#[tokio::test]
  1084→async fn clarify_as_project_first_action_is_linked_to_project() {
  1085→    let app = spawn_app().await;
  1086→    let client = register_and_login(
  1087→        &app.address,
  1088→        "projclarify6@example.com",
  1089→        "securepassword123",
  1090→    )
  1091→    .await;
  1092→
  1093→    let context_id = get_first_context_id(&client, &app.address).await;
  1094→
  1095→    // Capture an item
  1096→    client
  1097→        .post(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address))
  1098→        .form(&[("title", "Organize garage")])
  1099→        .send()
  1100→        .await
  1101→        .expect("Failed to capture");
  1102→
  1103→    let item_id = get_first_inbox_item_id(&client, &app.address).await;
  1104→
  1105→    // Clarify as project
  1106→    client
  1107→        .post(format!("{}/inbox/{}/clarify", &app.address, item_id))
  1108→        .form(&[
  1109→            ("clarify_type", "project"),
  1110→            ("context_id", &context_id),
  1111→            ("first_action_title", "Buy storage bins"),
  1112→        ])
  1113→        .send()
  1114→        .await
  1115→        .expect("Failed to clarify as project");
  1116→
  1117→    // Find the project ID from the projects page
  1118→    let response = client
  1119→        .get(format!("{}/projects", &app.address))
  1120→        .send()
  1121→        .await
  1122→        .expect("Failed to GET /projects");
  1123→    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
  1124→
  1125→    // Extract project ID from the project detail link
  1126→    let link_prefix = "href=\"/projects/";
  1127→    let start = body.find(link_prefix).expect("Missing project link") + link_prefix.len();
  1128→    let end = body[start..].find('"').expect("Missing closing quote");
  1129→    let project_id = &body[start..start + end];
  1130→
  1131→    // Project detail page should show the first action
  1132→    let response = client
  1133→        .get(format!("{}/projects/{}", &app.address, project_id))
  1134→        .send()
  1135→        .await
  1136→        .expect("Failed to GET project detail");
  1137→    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
  1138→    assert!(
  1139→        body.contains("Buy storage bins"),
  1140→        "Project detail should show the first action created during clarify"
  1141→    );
  1142→}
  1143→

content.text: Excellent. Now I have a thorough picture of the Step 7 implementation. Let me compile my review.

input.content:
**Step 7 (Clarify as Project) -- Accessibility Review**

I have reviewed the template, CSS, route handler, and integration tests.

**No BLOCKING items.**

This implementation follows all of the accessibility guidance I provided to Carson during the design discussion. Here is what I verified:

**Correct patterns applied:**

1. **Fieldset/Legend** (`inbox_item.html` lines 12-13): `<fieldset>` wraps the radio group and related fields. `<legend class="visually-hidden">Clarify: {{ item.title }}</legend>` gives screen reader users full context about what decision they are making. Correct.

2. **Radio buttons with visible labels** (lines 15-22): Wrapping `<label>` elements make the text a click/tap target and provide accessible names. `name="clarify_type"` groups them per form. "Next Action" is `checked` by default, which is the right default for the common case. Correct.

3. **Progressive disclosure via CSS `:has()`** (CSS line 1267-1276): Project fields use `display: none` by default and `display: flex` when `input[value="project"]:checked`. This correctly removes the fields from the accessibility tree when not relevant -- a screen reader in "Next Action" mode will not encounter the "First action" input. Correct.

4. **Visible label on first action input** (lines 33-34): `<label for="first-action-{{ item.id }}">First action</label>` with unique ID per inbox item. Correct.

5. **Visible label on context select** (lines 25-26): Carried forward from the existing pattern. `<label for="clarify-context-{{ item.id }}">Context</label>` with unique ID. Correct.

6. **Accessible name on submit button** (line 44): `aria-label="Clarify: {{ item.title }}"` -- distinguishes each button when multiple inbox items are on-screen. Correct.

7. **Trash remains a separate form** (lines 47-56): Destructive action is not folded into the radio group. Correct.

8. **No HTML `required` on first action input**: The input has no `required` attribute, and validation is server-side (`TodoTitle::parse` in the service, returning 422 on empty). This prevents the browser from blocking submission of a "Next Action" clarify due to a hidden required field. Correct.

9. **HTMX announce pattern** (route handler line 239): `htmx_response_with_announce("Clarified as project")` populates the live region. Correct.

10. **Focus management**: The existing `inbox-focus.js` already handles item removal after HTMX swap (outerHTML with empty body). The clarify-as-project response follows the same pattern (empty body, item removed), so focus management works without changes. Correct.

**Minor (non-blocking):**

- M1: The submit button text is a static "Clarify" regardless of which radio is selected. A screen reader user must remember which radio they checked before pressing "Clarify". A small JS progressive enhancement could make the button text dynamic ("Next Action" / "Create Project"), but the current `aria-label="Clarify: {{ item.title }}"` combined with the `<legend>` context is sufficient for WCAG compliance. Nice-to-have for a future polish pass.

**Integration tests** (lines 807-833): Tests verify the `aria-label` on both Clarify and Trash buttons, the presence of radio options, and the `first_action_title` input. Good coverage.

**CONSENSUS APPROVED** from Heydon Pickering (Accessibility).
input.summary: CONSENSUS APPROVED for Step 7 — no blocking items

6. **Accessible name on submit button** (line 44): `aria-label="Clarify: {{ item.title }}"` -- distinguishes each button when multiple inbox items are on-screen. Correct.

A small JS progressive enhancement could make the button text dynamic ("Next Action" / "Create Project"), but the current `aria-label="Clarify: {{ item.title }}"` combined with the `<legend>` context is sufficient for WCAG compliance. **Integration tests** (lines 807-833): Tests verify the `aria-label` on both Clarify and Trash buttons, the presence of radio options, and the `first_action_title` input. Good coverage.

**CONSENSUS APPROVED** from Heydon Pickering (Accessibility)."
  }
}

input.content:
**CONSENSUS APPROVED** from Heydon Pickering (Accessibility) for Step 7 (Clarify as Project).

No blocking items. The implementation correctly applies all accessibility patterns from the pre-implementation design discussion: fieldset/legend for the radio group, CSS `:has()` progressive disclosure with `display: none`, visible labels on all inputs, accessible names on buttons, server-side validation (no HTML `required` on conditionally-visible fields), and the existing inbox focus management script handles the new clarify path without changes.

One minor nice-to-have noted: dynamic button text based on radio selection (progressive enhancement).

No blocking items. The implementation correctly applies all accessibility patterns from the pre-implementation design discussion: fieldset/legend for the radio group, CSS `:has()` progressive disclosure with `display: none`, visible labels on all inputs, accessible names on buttons, server-side validation (no HTML `required` on conditionally-visible fields), and the existing inbox focus management script handles the new clarify path without changes.

One minor nice-to-have noted: dynamic button text based on radio selection (progressive enhancement).

content.text: Step 7 review complete. **CONSENSUS APPROVED** -- no blocking accessibility issues. The pre-implementation design discussion with Carson paid off: all 10 accessibility patterns I recommended were correctly implemented from the start, resulting in zero blocking items for the first time in the project. Sent approval to both Luca and team-lead.
